data.title: Altech Batteries Seeks Shareholder Approval for Security Arrangement
data.description:
Altech Chemicals Limited ( (AU:ATC) ) has shared an announcement.

Altech Batteries Limited has announced a General Meeting for shareholders to approve a security arrangement involving the company’s land in Johor, Malaysia. This move is part of a strategic effort to secure funding for ongoing projects and working capital needs without diluting equity. The company has entered into a binding bond note subscription deed with its major shareholder, Deutsche Balaton AG, to access up to €2.5 million through interest-bearing Bearer Bonds. The land sale proceeds will be used to repay this facility, which allows Altech to continue its development projects while maintaining shareholder value.

More about Altech Chemicals Limited

Altech Batteries Limited is a company operating in the battery industry, focusing on the development of advanced battery materials and technologies. The company’s primary projects include the CERENERGY battery project and the Silumina Anodes battery materials project, with a market focus on innovative energy storage solutions.

YTD Price Performance: -20.0%

Average Trading Volume: 1,274,463

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: A$74.1M

data.title: China Starch Holdings Announces 2025 AGM Agenda
data.description:
An announcement from China Starch Holdings Limited ( (HK:3838) ) is now available.

China Starch Holdings Limited has announced its upcoming annual general meeting (AGM) scheduled for May 20, 2025, in Hong Kong. The AGM will address several key business items, including the approval of the audited financial statements for 2024, declaration of a final dividend, re-election of directors, and re-appointment of the auditor. These actions are expected to impact the company’s governance and financial planning, potentially influencing stakeholder confidence and market positioning.

More about China Starch Holdings Limited

China Starch Holdings Limited is a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, primarily engaged in the starch industry. The company focuses on the production and sale of various starch products and related services.

data.title: MRG Metals Completes Infill Drilling at Mozambique Project
data.description:
MRG Metals Limited ( (AU:MRQ) ) just unveiled an announcement.

MRG Metals Limited has completed infill drilling at the Magonde target within the Marao HMS Project in Mozambique. The company has visually estimated the total heavy mineral content from panned samples and is awaiting laboratory analyses to confirm these findings. This development is part of MRG Metals’ ongoing efforts to advance its exploration activities and refine its understanding of the mineral resources at the site, potentially impacting its strategic positioning in the mineral sands market.

More about MRG Metals Limited

MRG Metals Limited operates in the mining industry, focusing on the exploration and development of mineral resources. The company is involved in projects that aim to identify and extract valuable minerals, with a particular emphasis on heavy mineral sands in Mozambique.

Current Market Cap: A$8.18M

data.title: Delta Air Lines Reports Solid March Quarter Results
data.description:
Delta Air Lines Inc. ( (DAL) ) has released its Q1 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Delta Air Lines Inc. presented to its investors.

Delta Air Lines Inc., a major player in the aviation industry, offers passenger and cargo air transportation services across six continents, known for its innovative customer service and strategic global partnerships. In its latest earnings report for the March quarter of 2025, Delta Air Lines announced a solid financial performance with operating revenue of $14 billion and an operating income of $569 million, maintaining profitability levels similar to the previous year. The company highlighted its strategic decision to reduce planned capacity growth in the second half of the year to align supply with demand amidst global economic uncertainties.

Key financial metrics from the report include an operating margin of 4.0% and earnings per share of $0.37. Delta’s diverse revenue streams, particularly in premium and loyalty segments, contributed significantly to its revenue growth, with international markets showing strong performance. The airline also reported a decrease in fuel expenses by 7% year-over-year, aiding its cost management efforts.

Delta’s balance sheet remains robust, with adjusted net debt reduced to $16.9 billion and a commitment to repay at least $3 billion of debt within the year. The company continues to invest in its fleet and customer experience, introducing new routes and enhancing in-flight services. Despite the current economic challenges, Delta’s strategic focus on cost management and revenue diversification positions it well for continued profitability.

Looking ahead, Delta Air Lines anticipates a June quarter operating margin between 11% and 14% and earnings per share ranging from $1.70 to $2.30. While the company refrains from providing a full-year outlook due to economic uncertainties, it remains confident in its ability to lead the industry in financial performance and maintain strong cash flow.
